textDisplay: With contrived plot points and forced diversity, the movie was slow, dull, clumsy, lackluster, and forgettable. There's no real tension and no memorable villians, and there's no chemistry between any of the characters. You're never invested in the characters to connect with them. Henry Cavill is once again a secondary character in his own movie. Henry Cavill walks through his performance and Alan Ritchson steals the movie. Henry Cavill and his group of characters were marketed heavily to be the central protagonists in the film, however, were onscreen for only 35 minutes in a two hour film. All the best parts of this movie were seen in the trailer. You'll have a better time just watching the trailer.
textOriginal: With contrived plot points and forced diversity, the movie was slow, dull, clumsy, lackluster, and forgettable. There's no real tension and no memorable villians, and there's no chemistry between any of the characters. You're never invested in the characters to connect with them. Henry Cavill is once again a secondary character in his own movie. Henry Cavill walks through his performance and Alan Ritchson steals the movie. Henry Cavill and his group of characters were marketed heavily to be the central protagonists in the film, however, were onscreen for only 35 minutes in a two hour film. All the best parts of this movie were seen in the trailer. You'll have a better time just watching the trailer.

*_Very interesting…_* Director and co-writer Guy Ritchie comes from a *_royal English family._* He apparently speaks *_Hebrew_* ‼️  and his children's names (from his second and current marriage) are *_Rafael, Rivka, and Levi._* (👃🏻👃🏻👃🏻)
He was previously married to *_Madonna._* 


*_Jerome Leon Bruckheimer,_* the well-known Hollywood executive producer, is the son of *_Jewish immigrants._* (👃🏻)


John *_Friedberg_* (👃🏻) co-producer. 


The movie is based on the 2014 book 'Churchill's Secret Warriors: The Explosive True Story of the Special Forces Desperadoes of WWII' by Damien Lewis. Lewis makes documentaries and writes books that many would describe as *_"woke, and very left-leaning"_* (climate change, human rights, systemic racism, etc). _That's nice..._


How fitting that they would portray "the *-good-* bad guys" in such a harsh tone. 🤔🙂

textDisplay:
Savaş filmlerini hep sevmişimdir. Guy Ritchie de bu işi bilenlerden... Henry Cavill biraz bebek suratlı olduğu için fazla garipsemedim aslında...Ritchie'nin filmleri genellikle unutulmaz ve "renkli" karakterler içerir; örneğin Snatch'teki İrlandalı boksör Mickey O'Neil ve Lock, Stock ve Two Smoking Barrels'daki suç patronu "Hatchet" Harry . Polisiye filmlerinde, birçok karakterin tehditkar davrandığı ve Cockney argosu kullandığı parçalanmış diyaloglar da var .  İngiliz sınıf sisteminin tasviri de araştırılmıştır. The Gentlemen'in kostüm tasarımcısı Michael Wilkinson , "Her karakter ikonik, akılda kalıcı bir görünüme sahip - hayattan biraz daha büyük" demiştir.

Hızlı tempolu ve enerjik aksiyon sahneleri , karakterlerini savaş veya şiddet içeren durumlara yerleştirmenin yanı sıra sinematik seçimler olarak da hizmet eder. Ritchie , hikayede ivme oluşturmak ve yüksek etkili bir izleme deneyimi yaratmak için sırasıyla hızlı kesme ve yavaş çekimi kullanmış . Ayrıca filmlerinde iç içe geçmiş hikayeler ve dairesel olay örgüsü gibi doğrusal olmayan bir anlatım kullandığı da biliniyor ; bu durum Lock, Stock ve Two Smoking Barrels, Sherlock Holmes ve Snatch'te görülür .
textOriginal:

Polisiye filmlerinde, birçok karakterin tehditkar davrandığı ve Cockney argosu kullandığı parçalanmış diyaloglar da var .  İngiliz sınıf sisteminin tasviri de araştırılmıştır. Hızlı tempolu ve enerjik aksiyon sahneleri , karakterlerini savaş veya şiddet içeren durumlara yerleştirmenin yanı sıra sinematik seçimler olarak da hizmet eder. Ritchie , hikayede ivme oluşturmak ve yüksek etkili bir izleme deneyimi yaratmak için sırasıyla hızlı kesme ve yavaş çekimi kullanmış . @hardluck8732  I didn't think I needed to elborate, but what I "want" to watch as a consumer, who is willing to pay real money to go to a real cinema with friends - is not another documentary, retelling of a true story or a political reflection of ideologies conflicting with modern cultures - but a stupid, silly, comedy with a black and white, good vs bad, simple plot.

I'm sick of woke and artistic garbage and just want some good all fashion, guilt free fun to watch and laugh with the boys about.

textDisplay: This mission is kinda true, but the first SAS weren't like this.  And there certainly weren't any americans in it. Sir David Stirling (a scot) started it all. Small groups of commandos, lightly armed and fast, went behind enemy lines and took out enemy fuel and ammo dumps in Libya and North Africa. And also point of fact, the americans didnt steal or recover the Enigma Machine from the germans.  The Royal navy did.  7 months before they entered the war.

textDisplay: The late great actor Sir Christopher Lee was in the actual unit during. WW2.He worked with Ritchie prior to his death Lee was decorated by every Allied country after the war but seldom spoke of his wartime service because he was bound by the Official Secrets Act.
textOriginal: The late great actor Sir Christopher Lee was in the actual unit during. WW2.He worked with Ritchie prior to his death Lee was decorated by every Allied country after the war but seldom spoke of his wartime service because he was bound by the Official Secrets Act.

textDisplay: Complete nonsense. Christopher Lee was an RAF intelligence officer with 260 Squadron who worked in aerial reconnaissance. No.62 Commando had already been disbanded by the time he returned from basic training in Rhodesia. He wasn't decorated and didn't serve in special forces besides giving the occasional briefing to SAS and SOE teams before missions while stationed in North Africa.
textOriginal: Complete nonsense. Christopher Lee was an RAF intelligence officer with 260 Squadron who worked in aerial reconnaissance. No.62 Commando had already been disbanded by the time he returned from basic training in Rhodesia. He wasn't decorated and didn't serve in special forces besides giving the occasional briefing to SAS and SOE teams before missions while stationed in North Africa.

textDisplay:
Something tells me that Guy isn't sticking with the facts of the source material... From a 2 minute  trailer I see so many things that are disappointing and obviously only changed an included to keep the woke Brigade happy.. During the 1940s the population of black people in Britain was 8,000 to 10,000 mostly around the ports and it went up and down in numbers all the time because over 50% of them were sailors and merchant seamen from mostly Dutch and other colony sailors, and none of them had British accents, and a lot couldn't be considered permanent residents.. Obviously the black population of Britain went up to 150,000 During ww2 but this was made up of black regiments from the US army and still doesn't account for a black soldier on this mission with a cockney accent! 
The female in the trailer! Yes there was women in the SOE but despite what people think, they were more intelligence gatherers and not running around duel holding pistols, and regardless, there were no women on Operation Postmaster! There was 11 men from the Small Scale Raiding Force, 4 men from the SOE and 17 volunteers from local men who took part, all men, all white or Spaniards.. 

I just don't know why the truth has to be changed to suit people's ridiculous woke views, yes many men of colours fought for the empire yes women were hero's too but the fact is the war was fought and won in a different time by different people.. And that was right for the time! I just think the truth and courage was amazing enough to be told to make a brilliant movie without changing things.. We need to realise this, we need to do better.

The trailer reminded me of the Amsterdam hype, everyone thought it would be a movie and...no.
So I don't know, it doesn't seem like a big deal. Typical film that you preferred to watch at home rather than spend time and money going to the cinema.


And there's Cavill... if there's Cavill, he's sure to fail at the box office. The man is an anchor for movies. Very rare exceptions.

textDisplay: The late great actor Sir Christopher Lee was in the actual unit during. WW2.He worked with Ritchie prior to his death Lee was decorated by every Allied country after the war but seldom spoke of his wartime service because he was bound by the Official Secrets Act.
textOriginal: The late great actor Sir Christopher Lee was in the actual unit during. WW2.He worked with Ritchie prior to his death Lee was decorated by every Allied country after the war but seldom spoke of his wartime service because he was bound by the Official Secrets Act.

textDisplay:
No he wasn't. Lee was an RAF intelligence officer with 260 Squadron. His job was interpreting aerial reconnaissance photos. 
He was never bound by the Official Secrets Act because those who did the most sensitive work during the war have been able to tell their stories. 
I suggest you read Gavin Mortimer's thorough takedown of Lee's war record.
textOriginal:
No he wasn't. Lee was an RAF intelligence officer with 260 Squadron. His job was interpreting aerial reconnaissance photos. 
He was never bound by the Official Secrets Act because those who did the most sensitive work during the war have been able to tell their stories. 
I suggest you read Gavin Mortimer's thorough takedown of Lee's war record.

textDisplay:
@blackstripes0812 No. Historian and expert on the SAS Gavin Mortimer did some digging into Lee's war record and found no evidence whatsoever that he was involved with frontline special forces operations beyond briefing SAS teams on aerial reconnaissance of enemy targets before missions while stationed in North Africa.
While Lee didn't straight out lie about his service, he was somewhat economical with the truth.
textOriginal:
@blackstripes0812 No. Historian and expert on the SAS Gavin Mortimer did some digging into Lee's war record and found no evidence whatsoever that he was involved with frontline special forces operations beyond briefing SAS teams on aerial reconnaissance of enemy targets before missions while stationed in North Africa.

textDisplay: Hope this movie has some actual good writing, im not rlly convinced yet by this 'look how crazy and wacky and deadly they are' Suicide Squad type deal, for fucks sake we haven't had a good WW2 mission film in years, especially not an exciting/adventure one like Guns of Navarone, or Where Eagles Dare, or Dirty Dozen, I love this cast but that's not a very high standard nowadays, every other action movie has casts like this. So please make this cool and exciting the way WW2 movies used to be, ik thats kinda controversial to say nowadays cuz war isn't exciting, its hell, and ofc thats true but there were actual fucking insanely incredible missions carried out by heroes, and its good to pay homage to that.

textDisplay:
It really is too bad that the admiralty got a little too sure of the ability of the SSRF and sent them on a raid in what would, 2 1/2 years later, be known as Omaha Beach in the D Day invasion of France. 

The missions were known as Operation Postmaster and Operation Aquatint, for anyone who would like to learn a little more history. 

There are two books I would recommend. The first is more of a traditional history narrative, "SOE
An Outline History of the Special Operations Executive, 1940-1946" by Michael Richard Daniell Foot, 1999.

The second is more of a studied report, written from a modern historic perspective, taking multiple narratives into account. This book is, "Special Operations Executive: A New Instrument of War," edited by Mark Seaman, 2013.
textOriginal:
It really is too bad that the admiralty got a little too sure of the ability of the SSRF and sent them on a raid in what would, 2 1/2 years later, be known as Omaha Beach in the D Day invasion of France. 

The missions were known as Operation Postmaster and Operation Aquatint, for anyone who would like to learn a little more history. 

There are two books I would recommend. The first is more of a traditional history narrative, "SOE
An Outline History of the Special Operations Executive, 1940-1946" by Michael Richard Daniell Foot, 1999.

The second is more of a studied report, written from a modern historic perspective, taking multiple narratives into account. This book is, "Special Operations Executive: A New Instrument of War," edited by Mark Seaman, 2013.

textDisplay:
@AnotherScribbler Unfortunately, Lee allowed people to think he did things that he didn't during WW2. As the intelligence officer with 260 Squadron he was occasionally asked to brief the SAS and SOE before missions using aerial reconnaissance photos. At no point did he take part in frontline operations. 

Ian Fleming was the real deal as he was the right hand man of the Chief of Naval Intelligence Admiral John Godfrey and sat on the Twenty Committee which involved working with SOE.
textOriginal:

textDisplay:

Another movie not worth crap.
textOriginal:
Well more woke garbage manipulation just to earn a seat at the award shows. The book and the original actual Special Forces teams never had any black or female members.

textDisplay:
I think you need to read up on your history.  The Special Operations executive had 41 female members working in the field (possibly more whose identities have never been revealed). Most of whom were posted in F-Section as courier spies. They were a vitally important part of turning the tide of the war.  There were also 3000 total female staff members out of 13,000 working for the organisation in general.
Many member of the SOE were from varying backgrounds and races, the organisation was known to employ people from different walks of life who had particularly unique skill sets.
One of the most famous SOE agents Noor Inayat Khan was a Muslim-Indian Princess.

Many member of the SOE were from varying backgrounds and races, the organisation was known to employ people from different walks of life who had particularly unique skill sets.
One of the most famous SOE agents Noor Inayat Khan was a Muslim-Indian Princess.

textDisplay: Would you like to hear some good news? This is the best news you’ll ever hear. All of us, without exception, have sinned and fall short of the glory of God (Romans 3:23). And because God is just and will not let sin go unpunished, the wages of sin is death (Romans 6:23). As a result, that means that as sinners, we will all get what we rightly deserve: eternal punishment, which is called the second death. But the good news is that God is loving and does not wish that any should perish but that all would come to repentance (2 Peter 3:9). So He made a way for us to be saved and now graciously offers you the free gift of eternal life through Jesus Christ (Romans 6:23), all while justice is done. He sent His Son, Jesus Christ, to die on behalf of us and pay for our sins on a cross, and He was then buried and rose again on the third day (1 Corinthians 15:1-4). Regardless of who you are or what you may have done, if you believe with your heart the gospel ( ^^ which I just mentioned above) and confess with your mouth that Jesus is Lord, you shall be saved (Romans 10:9,10). Salvation is simple and free. It’s not a 12 step program, and don’t think, as many do, that you just have to be a “good person” or do good works to go to heaven. Salvation is based on what Jesus did, not what you do (Ephesians 2:8,9). Believe in Christ, and you will be saved. “For God so loved the world that He gave His only begotten Son that whosoever should believe in Him shall not perish but have eternal life. For God did not send His Son into the world to condemn the world but to save the world through Him.” John 3:16,17
textOriginal: Would you like to hear some good news? This is the best news you’ll ever hear. All of us, without exception, have sinned and fall short of the glory of God (Romans 3:23). And because God is just and will not let sin go unpunished, the wages of sin is death (Romans 6:23). As a result, that means that as sinners, we will all get what we rightly deserve: eternal punishment, which is called the second death. But the good news is that God is loving and does not wish that any should perish but that all would come to repentance (2 Peter 3:9). So He made a way for us to be saved and now graciously offers you the free gift of eternal life through Jesus Christ (Romans 6:23), all while justice is done. He sent His Son, Jesus Christ, to die on behalf of us and pay for our sins on a cross, and He was then buried and rose again on the third day (1 Corinthians 15:1-4). Regardless of who you are or what you may have done, if you believe with your heart the gospel ( ^^ which I just mentioned above) and confess with your mouth that Jesus is Lord, you shall be saved (Romans 10:9,10). Salvation is simple and free. It’s not a 12 step program, and don’t think, as many do, that you just have to be a “good person” or do good works to go to heaven. Salvation is based on what Jesus did, not what you do (Ephesians 2:8,9). Believe in Christ, and you will be saved. “For God so loved the world that He gave His only begotten Son that whosoever should believe in Him shall not perish but have eternal life. For God did not send His Son into the world to condemn the world but to save the world through Him.” John 3:16,17

textDisplay:
as we are propelled into a national situation resembling the Weimar more and more with each passing day, it's crucial for them to keep reminding us how bad they want us to believe the National Socialists were. 

I prefer to draw my opinions from actual books regarding the Bolsheviks, The Weimar Republic, The Treaty of Versailles, the attempted communist revolution in Germany, the miraculous economic recovery that Hitl3r navigated, and the writings of Churchill before the chancellor made any military decisions, especially  bragging that Britain was planning to militarily crush Germany, irrespective of anything Germany did, and also the diaries of George Patton make interesting reading. 

Are you being entertained?

textDisplay: Unfortunately, this film is "based on a true story" almost as much as the Fargo TV series. Sure, there was a mission involving Ian Fleming, but it was nothing like the story in this film, and it didn't involve any Wokecasting either. The heroic soldiers were all white men, but this "reenvisioning" has made it a multi-gender all-colours-of-the rainbow virtual signalling affair. At least the film advertises itself as a comedy, which is appropriate, given how laughable it it.
textOriginal: Unfortunately, this film is "based on a true story" almost as much as the Fargo TV series. Sure, there was a mission involving Ian Fleming, but it was nothing like the story in this film, and it didn't involve any Wokecasting either. The heroic soldiers were all white men, but this "reenvisioning" has made it a multi-gender all-colours-of-the rainbow virtual signalling affair. At least the film advertises itself as a comedy, which is appropriate, given how laughable it it.

textDisplay:
Looks too stylistic and possibly a "snooze fest" if there's no good writing or story telling. 

Often what sets QT [i.e. Inglorious Bastards, Kill Bill] apart from other directories is the excellent writing, well-placed pauses and artistic greatness.

Guy Ritchie has made a few poor installments as of late, and I'm a fan. Henry Cavil is a solid lead. 

Could go either way. 

You'll know in the first act 🎬  if it's a good watch.

What do I think are great movies in this genre? [Pacing]

Snatch, Boondock Saints, Fight Club, Lucky Number Sleven, The Boys [Season 1]

Fingers crossed 🤞

textDisplay:
Woah. So a movie about a ragtag team of inclusive characters with quirky personality traits that are decimating a battalion of goofy and inept Nazis all made into a story "based on true events" that no one knows about, but still important enough that it helped change the outcome of WW2 for the better.

I'm glad that Hollywood finally has some fresh ideas to serve us...

textDisplay: @diegosalvatore6871 he was one of the turtles in the movie, and he was talking about how they were treated poorly and lied to about the amount of exposure they’d get. Obviously being in a costume for the whole movie, where the audience can’t see your face isn’t great for an actor. But they were told they would be pushed in the media, and in the marketing so people would know who they were, but it never happened. He also spoke about how they’d always be the last to leave set because they were on a fixed wage, whereas the crew and other actors would get paid overtime, so to avoid paying them overtime, they would make them get on the buses first, and leave the turtles actors waiting hours for a ride back to the hotel…because they weren’t paid overtime
textOriginal: @diegosalvatore6871 he was one of the turtles in the movie, and he was talking about how they were treated poorly and lied to about the amount of exposure they’d get. Obviously being in a costume for the whole movie, where the audience can’t see your face isn’t great for an actor. But they were told they would be pushed in the media, and in the marketing so people would know who they were, but it never happened. He also spoke about how they’d always be the last to leave set because they were on a fixed wage, whereas the crew and other actors would get paid overtime, so to avoid paying them overtime, they would make them get on the buses first, and leave the turtles actors waiting hours for a ride back to the hotel…because they weren’t paid overtime

textDisplay:
Eh I just finished watching "SAS - Rogue Heroes" which is a retelling of the ACTUAL first airfield raids, this film is very, very, very inaccurate, I can already tell by the trailer. But I clicked it anyway, because I love sailboats.

The trailer looks shit. The music doesn't fit neither the theme nor the timeframe, and even though we all love us some Freddie, it's totally overused.

Also what's wrong with Henry Cavills tongue? It seems to stick out all the time.

Besides, we got all the tropes: The femme fatale who can fight better than any man, the token black, main character being a "funny", wisecracking petty person without manners (like pretty much every major character in films these days..... Just think Guardians of the Galaxy.). 

Looks like they had a very mediocre screenwriter but a very talented producer, who could claim all the big names to sell this. Because, let's face it, this film will not flop. Might not be the best film you've ever seen, but it will make a few million and everyone is happy then.
textOriginal:
Eh I just finished watching "SAS - Rogue Heroes" which is a retelling of the ACTUAL first airfield raids, this film is very, very, very inaccurate, I can already tell by the trailer. But I clicked it anyway, because I love sailboats.

The trailer looks shit. The music doesn't fit neither the theme nor the timeframe, and even though we all love us some Freddie, it's totally overused.

Also what's wrong with Henry Cavills tongue? It seems to stick out all the time.

Besides, we got all the tropes: The femme fatale who can fight better than any man, the token black, main character being a "funny", wisecracking petty person without manners (like pretty much every major character in films these days..... Just think Guardians of the Galaxy.). 

Looks like they had a very mediocre screenwriter but a very talented producer, who could claim all the big names to sell this. Because, let's face it, this film will not flop. Might not be the best film you've ever seen, but it will make a few million and everyone is happy then.

textDisplay:
This movie is based on a book of the same name that covers the history of The Special Operations Executive. It was Britain's Special Forces agency. Founded at a time when Britain was all alone and Germany controlled all of Europe. 

Churchill told The SOE to "set Europe ablaze."
